name: Ziggurat
description: Quad voltage quantizer for turning CV and gates into pitch. Four independent channels with per-channel masks, slew, root, offset, range and chained note-sequence modes. 14HP, 25mm deep.

controls.name: Channel Buttons 1-4
controls.description: Select and edit one of the four quantizer channels.
controls.detail: hold two or more for 1s to link

controls.name: One-Octave Note Mask
controls.description: Keyboard-style button grid. Turns each chromatic note on or off to build the quantization mask.

controls.name: Save / Load
controls.description: Stores and recalls the complete state of all four channels — masks, slew, root, offset, range and chains.

controls.name: Slew
controls.description: Per-note slew — you can enable slew on individual notes inside the mask so only chosen pitches glide.

controls.name: Root
controls.description: Shifts the channel output by a semitone offset to change musical key.

controls.name: Offset
controls.description: Offsets the input CV by semitones before quantization — transposes where the input lands on the mask.

controls.name: Range
controls.description: Sets the output voltage span. Press the lower then upper bound of the keyboard to define it.

controls.name: Chain
controls.description: Turns the channel into an SH-101 style step sequencer — enter a series of notes that advance on gate or scan with CV.

controls.name: Mod Assign
controls.description: Routes the Mod CV input to any of the main edit parameters for hands-free modulation.

inputs.name: Channel In 1-4
inputs.description: Four independent CV/gate inputs — each one is quantized by its matching channel.

inputs.name: Chain Gate In
inputs.description: When the selected channel is in Chain mode, each gate advances the chain by one note.

inputs.name: Chain CV In
inputs.description: Scans through the chain sequence bidirectionally — 0V plays the first note, +5V plays the last.

inputs.name: Mod CV In
inputs.description: Dynamic CV input routed by Mod Assign to slew, root, offset, range or load.

outputs.name: Channel Out 1-4
outputs.description: Four quantized pitch outputs, one per channel. Output span set by each channel's Range setting.

behaviors.name: Independent quantizer channels
behaviors.trigger: route a CV source into a channel input
behaviors.description: Each of the four channels quantizes independently with its own mask, root, offset, range, slew and chain — one module can drive four coordinated pitch lanes.

behaviors.name: Custom note masks
behaviors.description: Per-channel note masks define which chromatic notes are available. Channels can move between sparse scales, chromatic masks, or a hand-built melodic note set.

behaviors.name: Channel linking
behaviors.trigger: hold two or more channel buttons for 1 second
behaviors.description: Linked channels share parameter edits, so you can build chordal or harmonized behaviors without recreating the same mask in each channel.

behaviors.name: Per-note slew
behaviors.trigger: enable slew on a note in the mask
behaviors.description: Slew is assigned per note rather than per channel — glide only the chosen pitches, leaving the rest to snap. Useful for hammer-on-style melodies where only certain intervals bend.

behaviors.name: Chain sequencer mode
behaviors.trigger: enter Chain mode on a channel
behaviors.description: Chain mode replaces the quantizer with an SH-101 style note sequence. A gate at Chain Gate In advances one step; a 0–5V CV at Chain CV In scans the entire sequence in either direction.

behaviors.name: Assignable Mod CV
behaviors.trigger: assign Mod CV to a parameter
behaviors.description: One Mod CV input routes to any main parameter (load, slew, root, offset, range). That lets you modulate masks, keys or even recall presets without adding sequencers.

behaviors.name: Stored presets
behaviors.description: 35 preset slots in 5 banks of 7 remember the complete module state — every mask, range, chain and assignment — so a performer can jump between full setups instantly.

patchIdeas.name: Four-channel pitch quantizer
patchIdeas.patch: Four CV sources into Channel In 1-4, each channel masked to a related scale, Channel Out 1-4 driving four oscillators — classic four-voice quantized harmony from one module.
steps: Patch Random/LFO/Seq source 1 → Ziggurat Channel In 1, source 2 → Channel In 2, source 3 → Channel In 3, source 4 → Channel In 4.
Press Channel Button 1 — on the one-octave keyboard, enable the notes C, D, E, G, A (major pentatonic).
Press Channel Button 2 — enable C, Eb, F, G, Bb (minor pentatonic).
Press Channel Button 3 — enable the same pentatonic as channel 2 and set Root to +7 semitones for a fifth above.
Press Channel Button 4 — enable all 12 notes (chromatic) for a free lane.
Set each channel's Range to 2 octaves so voices stay in a usable register.
Patch Channel Out 1 → VCO 1 V/Oct, Channel Out 2 → VCO 2 V/Oct, Channel Out 3 → VCO 3 V/Oct, Channel Out 4 → VCO 4 V/Oct.
Patch each VCO → its own VCA/filter → mixer → output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Channel Out 1-4 — four 1V/oct pitch CVs, each spanning up to 12 octaves of –4V to +8V.
patchIdeas.hear: Four simultaneous voices that all fit together harmonically: random wobbles on lane 4 stay chromatic glue while lanes 1-3 lock into major/minor pentatonic territory. Retune a root to shift the whole stack into a new key.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ziggurat:
* CH1 mask: major pentatonic
* CH2 mask: minor pentatonic
* CH3 mask: minor pent + Root +7
* CH4 mask: chromatic

CV1:
CV2:
CV3:
CV4:
VCO1:
VCO2:
VCO3:
VCO4:
Mixer:

- CV1 (Out) >> Ziggurat (Channel In 1)
- CV2 (Out) >> Ziggurat (Channel In 2)
- CV3 (Out) >> Ziggurat (Channel In 3)
- CV4 (Out) >> Ziggurat (Channel In 4)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 1) p> VCO1 (V/Oct)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 2) p> VCO2 (V/Oct)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 3) p> VCO3 (V/Oct)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 4) p> VCO4 (V/Oct)
- VCO1 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCO2 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCO3 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCO4 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Slewed mask melody
patchIdeas.patch: Random CV into one channel, per-note slew on selected pitches, root shifted to taste — a melody that glides between certain notes and snaps between others.
steps: Patch a slow random/LFO CV source → Ziggurat Channel In 1.
Press Channel Button 1 — enable C, D, E, G, A on the one-octave keyboard.
Enter the Slew menu and turn slew ON for the notes D and G only — about 12:00 (roughly 1-2 seconds glide).
Set Root to +5 semitones to center the lane on F.
Set Range to 2 octaves so the melody sits in a playable register.
Patch Ziggurat Channel Out 1 → VCO V/Oct.
Patch VCO (sawtooth) → VCA (In).
Trigger a gate source → Envelope → VCA CV for note articulation.
Patch VCA (Out) → your mixer or output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Channel Out 1 — a 1V/oct pitch CV that snaps between most mask notes but glides smoothly through D and G.
patchIdeas.hear: A half-robotic, half-vocal melody: most notes click into place, but every time the sequence lands on D or G the pitch scoops into it. Turning slew on across more notes turns the line into a lyra-like glide.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ziggurat:
* CH1 mask: C D E G A (slew on D, G)
* Root: +5
* Range: 2 oct

Random:
VCO:
Env:
VCA:

- Random (Out) >> Ziggurat (Channel In 1)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Saw) -> VCA (In)
- Gate (Trig) g> Env (Trig)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: SH-101 style chain
patchIdeas.patch: Chain mode turns channel 1 into a stepped melody sequencer — a clock advances the notes while an external 0-5V CV can scan the sequence forward or backward.
steps: Press Channel Button 1 and enter Chain mode on the channel.
Enter a sequence of notes by tapping the keyboard — e.g. C, E, G, B, A, D, C, F (8 notes).
Set Range to 2 octaves so jumps stay musical.
Patch a clock → Ziggurat Chain Gate In — each clock advances the chain by one note.
Optional: patch a slow LFO or envelope (0-5V) → Ziggurat Chain CV In to scan the whole sequence; 0V plays the first note, +5V plays the last.
Patch Ziggurat Channel Out 1 → VCO V/Oct.
Patch VCO → filter → VCA → output.
Mult the same clock → your envelope trigger for per-step articulation.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Channel Out 1 — a stepped pitch CV that advances through the programmed Chain notes, 1V/oct.
patchIdeas.hear: A classic SH-101-style melodic run: each clock pulse hops to the next programmed note. Replace the clock with an LFO on the Chain CV In and the sequence sweeps back and forth like a tape scrub.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ziggurat:
* CH1 Chain: C E G B A D C F
* Range: 2 oct

Clock:
LFO:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) g> Ziggurat (Chain Gate In)
- LFO (Out) >> Ziggurat (Chain CV In) // optional scan
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Clock (Out) g> Env (Trig)
- Env (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Linked harmony pair
patchIdeas.patch: Link channels 1 and 2 so they share a mask; offset channel 2's Root by a third for a parallel harmony voice while channels 3 and 4 remain free for bass and texture.
steps: Press Channel Button 1 — build a major scale mask (C D E F G A B).
Hold Channel Button 1 and Channel Button 2 together for 1 second — the LEDs confirm the channels are linked.
Release; channel 2 now mirrors channel 1's mask and edits.
Select channel 2 alone and set Root to +4 semitones — a major third above channel 1.
Patch your sequencer pitch CV → Ziggurat Channel In 1, and mult the same CV → Channel In 2.
Patch Channel Out 1 → VCO 1 V/Oct, Channel Out 2 → VCO 2 V/Oct.
Assign Mod CV to Root and patch a slow LFO → Ziggurat Mod CV In to modulate the shared key.
Send both VCOs to a mixer → output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Channel Out 1 + Channel Out 2 — two 1V/oct pitch CVs in a parallel harmonic interval.
patchIdeas.hear: A constant major-third harmony above your melody that stays in-scale because both lanes share the same mask. The slow LFO on Root drifts the key by semitones, so the harmony transposes as a unit without losing tuning.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ziggurat:
* CH1+CH2 linked, major mask
* CH2 Root: +4
* Mod Assign: Root

Seq:
LFO:
VCO1:
VCO2:
Mixer:

- Seq (Pitch) p> Ziggurat (Channel In 1)
- Seq (Pitch) p> Ziggurat (Channel In 2) // multed
- LFO (Out) >> Ziggurat (Mod CV In)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 1) p> VCO1 (V/Oct)
- Ziggurat (Channel Out 2) p> VCO2 (V/Oct)
- VCO1 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- VCO2 (Out) -> Mixer (In)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)
